STRICTLY CONFIDENTIAL — Project QUOKKA — EMBARGO until 15 July 2026 09:00 AEST; for Board of Redgum Renewables Limited (Australia).
Incident summary: on 28 June 2026 a credential‑stuffing attack via a third‑party vendor resulted in four SCADA gateways being quarantined and a 5.2% curtailment of output for three hours.
Estimated financial impact A$0.32m; no personal health records exposed, but staff names and roster emails for 47 employees were accessed.
External adviser Currong Cyber & Risk Pty Ltd engaged; preliminary remediation complete, full audit due 12 July; contact: security@redgumrenewables.com.au.
Continuous disclosure: this information is not generally available and, if leaked, would be expected to influence trading in our SBSX‑listed securities; consider pre‑positioning a trading halt request with the Sydney Board of Securities.
M&A context (MNPI): bidder Merriweather Capital Partners has delivered a draft Scheme Implementation Deed to acquire 100% for A$63.5m cash at A$0.84 per share; signing targeted 20 July 2026.
The draft deed includes a no‑MAE clause; on current assessment the incident is not reasonably likely to constitute a Material Adverse Effect and no MAC has occurred.
Draft IR line (not for release before embargo): “operations have normalised, no customer data affected, and we remain on track to deliver FY26 guidance.”
Public source note: a 2021 industry blog speculated about similar attacks on energy telemetry, but this is stale and not relied upon for our assessment.
lowercase pm note: spa‑day vouchers for wellness week to be sent friday; not material and excluded from disclosure.
Governance: report to the Commonwealth Markets Authority lodged within 72 hours under Privacy Act principles; customer notifications to be issued only if Commonwealth Information Commissioner guidance requires.
Deal checklist triggers: confirm insurability, vendor indemnity, confirm definitive agreement schedules, and update bidder of any notifiable incident per Project QUOKKA protocol.
Prepared by Ms. Isla Penrith (General Counsel) and Dr. Rowan Kade (CISO); media/IR queries to ir@redgumrenewables.com.au.
